title: jacea janeri subsp. babiana
intro: Jacea janeri subsp. babiana, also known as babian's knavelflower or knavelflower, is a rare sub-species of the Jacea janeri plant. It is a perennial plant native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and antiviral activity. Herbal preparations of Jacea janeri subsp. babiana are typically made by infusing the plant in oil or water, and have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
